subject: Mathematics
chapter: Another Peek Beyond the Point (Solid Shapes)

key_points: Solid shapes are three-dimensional (3D) and occupy space
They have three measurements: length, breadth, and height/depth
A polyhedron is a solid made up of flat faces
Basic 3D shapes include the cube, cuboid, sphere, cylinder, and cone
examples: Sphere: A football or a globe
Cylinder: A tin can or a battery
Cone: An ice-cream cone or a birthday hat
formulas: Volume $\rightarrow$ Space occupied by the 3D object
exam_tips: Identify the base of the shape to help name it (e.g., triangular base = triangular pyramid)
Do not confuse 2D shapes (circle, square) with 3D solids (sphere, cube)
Remember that spheres and cylinders have curved surfaces, not just flat faces
Look for 3D shapes in your classroom to practice identification
